content.full_text:
•

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

January 24, 2005

Elizabeth Wagner Hull
Assistant City Attorney
City of Chula Vista
276 Fourth Avenue
Chula Vista, CA 91910

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-241

Dear Ms. Hull:

The purpose of this letter is to confirm your request (made during our telephone
conversations of December 15, 2005 and January 18, 2006), to withdraw your prior letter
(dated November 23, 2005) requesting advice regarding the Political Reform Act (the
"Act") on behalf of Councilmember Steve Castaneda.

That letter contained two questions. With regard to the first question,
Councilmember Castaneda first informed me by telephone on December 12, 2005 that
the council meeting at which the relevant governmental vote was to take place was going
to occur on December 13. I informed him that we would be unable to provide him with
any advice on such short notice. On December 15, 2005, I confirmed with you by phone
that the vote had taken place on December 13, 2005; therefore, you agreed to withdraw
the first question since we cannot provide advice regarding past conduct. (See
Regulation 18329(b)(8) copy enclosed.)

You indicated to me in our January 18, 2006 conversation that you wished to
voluntarily withdraw your request for advice regarding the second question because the
facts have been revised. (Note: the revised factual scenario for which you seek advice is
contained in a subsequent letter we recently received from you, Our File No. 06-003.)

File No. W-05-241
Page No. 2

If you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Andreas C. Rockas
Counsel, Legal Division

AR:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\05-241

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city councilmember's conduct regarding governmental votes and related matters complied with the Political Reform Act (two original questions were submitted but subsequently withdrawn).
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms withdrawal of both questions: the first question regarding past conduct cannot be advised upon, and the second question was voluntarily withdrawn by the requestor due to revised facts being addressed in a subsequent letter (File No. 06-003).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter confirming withdrawal of two original questions from prior advice request. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; document confirms procedural withdrawal details. Date discrepancy noted in document (January 24, 2005 letterhead but references December 2005 and January 2006 dates in body text, suggesting possible dating error in original document).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a city councilmember's conduct regarding governmental votes and related matters complied with the Political Reform Act (two original questions were submitted but subsequently withdrawn).

CONCLUSION: The FPPC confirms withdrawal of both questions: the first question regarding past conduct cannot be advised upon, and the second question was voluntarily withdrawn by the requestor due to revised facts being addressed in a subsequent letter ().

embedding.summary: This is a withdrawal letter confirming the City of Chula Vista's request to withdraw an advice letter dated November 23, 2005, regarding Councilmember Steve Castaneda's compliance with the Political Reform Act; the first question was withdrawn because the relevant vote had already occurred (making retroactive advice unavailable), and the second question was voluntarily withdrawn due to revised factual circumstances.
